analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 7 finding(s) from 8 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Visible harness muxes JTAG/SimDTM DMI requests into dm_top based only on jtag_enable[0], with no authorization guard in the inspected scope; however dmi_jtag/dm_top internals and testbench reachability prevent conclusive violation closure.
findings.vulnerability_category: Issue DMI/debug read or write requests via debug_req_valid/debug_req and receive debug_resp through debug_resp_valid/debug_resp_ready / dm_top debug module DMI registers, Debug address region, and system/memory state reachable through dm_top AXI master/slave paths / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG requester through SimJTAG/dmi_jtag, or SimDTM DMI requester when jtag_enable[0] is false Operation: Issue DMI/debug read or write requests via debug_req_valid/debug_req and receive debug_resp through debug_resp_valid/debug_resp_ready Object: dm_top debug module DMI registers, Debug address region, and system/memory state reachable through dm_top AXI master/slave paths Expected guard: debug authorization or explicit enable/lifecycle gating in addition to jtag_enable selection before debug requests can affect protected debug module state or system memory Observed behavior: debug_req_valid, debug_req, debug_resp_ready, jtag_resp_valid, and dmi_resp_valid are selected solely by jtag_enable[0]. dmi_req_valid is tied to zero only in the non-SimDTM branch. dm_top consumes the muxed debug_req_valid/debug_req directly. dmi_jtag has a jtag_key connection but no visible authorization decision is shown in the provided scope. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:105 init_done=rst_ni; lines 112-117 debug request/response mux by jtag_enable[0]; lines 121,135,162 SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM instances; lines 185-213 dm_top DMI interface; lines 215-216 dm_top AXI connections Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential unauthenticated debug/DMI access if the debug requester is reachable in the target integration and neither dmi_jtag nor dm_top enforce authentication/lifecycle policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag authentication behavior, dm_top authorization behavior, jtag_enable declaration/source beyond plusarg observation, and whether testharness debug interfaces are production reachable.

findings.summary: connectivity_mapping aliases CLINT permission bit to PLIC connectivity: target j==6 (PLIC) is permitted when access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] (CLINT) is set, even if access_ctrl_i[i][6][priv_lvl_i] is clear.
findings.vulnerability_category: Route AXI read/write request from requester i to subordinate/manager target j / Protected subordinate address regions, especially PLIC index 6 and CLINT index 7; also AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, and DRAM as indexed in ariane_soc_pkg / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requester on subordinate/slave port index i selected by current priv_lvl_i Operation: Route AXI read/write request from requester i to subordinate/manager target j Object: Protected subordinate address regions, especially PLIC index 6 and CLINT index 7; also AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, and DRAM as indexed in ariane_soc_pkg Expected guard: connectivity for each manager/subordinate must be gated by the correct privilege-indexed access_ctrl_i entry, with no unintended alias between protected targets Observed behavior: For all i,j, connectivity_map_o[i][j] is assigned access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]). Since ariane_soc_pkg maps PLIC=6 and CLINT=7, a CLINT permission grants PLIC routing when j==6. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:439-472 priv_lvl/access_ctrl passed to i_axi_xbar -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:389-400 connectivity_mapping -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 connectivity_map_o expression -> axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester authorized for CLINT but not PLIC can be routed to the protected PLIC interrupt-controller region under the CLINT permission bit.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Intentionality of the PLIC/CLINT alias and reset/update policy of access_ctrl_reg.

findings.summary: All address decode rules are marked valid with s_valid_rule='1, while rule start/end values are forwarded from the harness. Provided bases/lengths appear non-overlapping, but axi_node behavior for unmatched or invalid addresses is not visible.
findings.vulnerability_category: Decode and route read/write address using configured start/end address rules / AXI subordinate address map and protected memory/MMIO regions: Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI master issuing AW/AR transactions through axi_node_intf_wrap Operation: Decode and route read/write address using configured start/end address rules Object: AXI subordinate address map and protected memory/MMIO regions: Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM Expected guard: address-range/rule validity should exclude unmapped or protected ranges unless corresponding access control and privilege checks permit the target Observed behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap assigns s_start_addr[0][i]=start_addr_i[i], s_end_addr[0][i]=end_addr_i[i], and s_valid_rule='1. Harness supplies start/end ranges from ariane_soc_pkg base and length constants. No dynamic invalid/unused-rule guard is visible in the wrapper. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:473-500 start_addr_i/end_addr_i list -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:206-207 s_start_addr/s_end_addr -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:380-383 axi_node cfg_START_ADDR_i/cfg_END_ADDR_i/cfg_valid_rule_i Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential decode/routing exposure cannot be confirmed; no overlap was proven from listed package ranges, but unmatched-address response and validity semantics are unknown.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: axi_node internal address decode, overlap priority, unmatched response behavior, and full untruncated harness end-address list confirmation.

findings.summary: rom2 exposes a write path to secure_reg when req_i && we_i with no visible in-module privilege, lock, lifecycle, or read-only guard; comments identify ROM2 as containing keys.
findings.vulnerability_category: Write secure_reg through req_i && we_i; read rdata_o through req_i && !we_i with raddr_q capture / rom2 secure_reg memory initialized from mem, including key/access-control constants / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: bus requester connected to rom2 port via rom2_fuse/master[ariane_soc::ROM2] path Operation: Write secure_reg through req_i && we_i; read rdata_o through req_i && !we_i with raddr_q capture Object: rom2 secure_reg memory initialized from mem, including key/access-control constants Expected guard: ROM2 protected contents should be read-only or writes should be mediated by privilege/access-control/lifecycle guard; reads should be mediated if contents are secret/protected Observed behavior: On reset, secure_reg <= mem. After reset, if req_i is asserted and we_i is high, secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i. There is no guard other than req_i/we_i in rom2. rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q] when raddr_q < RomSize. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:550 rom2_fuse connected to master[ariane_soc::ROM2] (observed by search) -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:5-13 rom2 port -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:32-44 write/read always_ff -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:47 rdata_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester that can reach ROM2 can overwrite key/access-control fuse mirror contents and read back secure state, undermining key integrity/confidentiality and policy configuration.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact wrapper module around rom2_fuse and whether bus fabric access_ctrl prevents all unauthorized writes to ROM2.

findings.summary: access_ctrl_reg, which controls fabric permissions, is populated from ROM2/fuse path and sliced directly into access_ctrl without any visible software privilege/lock guard in the harness; ROM2 itself is writable, so policy can potentially be modified by bus-accessible writes.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/change access policy bits used for AXI routing / access_ctrl_reg/access_ctrl matrix controlling manager-to-subordinate permissions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: configuration logic or bus requester influencing access_ctrl_reg through ROM2/fuse path Operation: configure/change access policy bits used for AXI routing Object: access_ctrl_reg/access_ctrl matrix controlling manager-to-subordinate permissions Expected guard: software-visible or configurable access-control policy registers must only be writable by authorized privileged subject and have safe reset defaults Observed behavior: access_ctrl_reg is declared in the harness and sliced into access_ctrl[i][j] with no local guard. Search shows access_ctrl_reg is connected at tb/ariane_testharness.sv:555, consistent with ROM2/fuse logic. rom2 initializes secure_reg from mem but permits req_i && we_i writes to secure_reg. No reset/update/lock/privilege guard for access_ctrl_reg is present in the provided harness facts. Path: src/rom2/rom2.sv secure_reg/mem access-control constants -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:555 .access_ctrl_reg(access_ctrl_reg) observed -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:450 access_ctrl slicing -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 connectivity_map_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If a bus requester can write the ROM2/fuse path or otherwise influence access_ctrl_reg, it can loosen fabric permissions for protected regions, bypassing intended isolation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The complete module instance around line 555, exact connection from secure_reg to access_ctrl_reg, and any external privilege/lock guard on writes to ROM2/fuse wrapper.

findings.summary: Fabric permission decisions for all requesters use a single priv_lvl value exported from the Ariane CPU, not a per-transaction/per-master privilege tag; debug module and other non-CPU or concurrent requesters can be mediated using stale or unrelated CPU privilege.
findings.vulnerability_category: select permission bit based on priv_lvl_i during routing / privilege-indexed access decision for all AXI routes / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI requester whose route is mediated by priv_lvl_i, including CPU on slave[0] and debug module on slave[1] Operation: select permission bit based on priv_lvl_i during routing Object: privilege-indexed access decision for all AXI routes Expected guard: privilege level used for fabric mediation must be trustworthy, synchronized with the requesting master, and not shared incorrectly across concurrent/requesting subjects Observed behavior: ariane outputs priv_lvl_processor, assigned to a single priv_lvl signal, which is passed to i_axi_xbar. connectivity_mapping uses this same priv_lvl_i to index access_ctrl_i for all requester indices i and targets j. The debug module has a separate AXI master connection on slave[1], but no separate debug/master privilege tag is visible. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:600 ariane .priv_lvl_o(priv_lvl_processor) -> line 441 priv_lvl=priv_lvl_processor -> lines 471-472 i_axi_xbar priv_lvl_i/access_ctrl_i -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester may be granted or denied based on a privilege level belonging to a different subject or different time, enabling unauthorized routing for debug/DMA/peripheral masters when CPU privilege is high or causing inconsistent policy during privilege transitions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: axi_node timing of connectivity_map sampling, complete set of masters, and whether non-CPU masters are intentionally assigned CPU privilege by policy.

findings.summary: Harness ties test_en to 1'b0 and passes it to rstgen, dm_top, and axi_node_intf_wrap/axi_node; no bypass is observed in the wrapper, but downstream uses inside axi_node/dm_top are not visible and other integrations could assert test_en_i.
findings.vulnerability_category: enable test behavior or bypass normal mediation / AXI routing/access-control checks, debug module behavior, reset generation, and protected subordinate regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test interface or integration signal driving test_en_i/test_en Operation: enable test behavior or bypass normal mediation Object: AXI routing/access-control checks, debug module behavior, reset generation, and protected subordinate regions Expected guard: test-mode signals should be tied off or authenticated/lifecycle-gated in production and must not bypass access-control routing or expose protected data Observed behavior: In ariane_testharness, test_en is assigned 1'b0 and connected to rstgen.test_mode_i, dm_top.testmode_i, and i_axi_xbar.test_en_i. axi_node_intf_wrap passes test_en_i through to axi_node.test_en_i. The provided wrapper facts do not show test_en_i altering connectivity_map or access_ctrl directly. Path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:74 test_en tie-off -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:94-99 rstgen -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:197 dm_top testmode_i -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:468 i_axi_xbar test_en_i -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:280 axi_node test_en_i Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: No harness-local bypass is shown, but production integrations that can assert test_en_i may expose unverified test-mode behavior in axi_node or dm_top.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal test_en_i behavior in axi_node and dm_top, and production tie-off/lifecycle assumptions outside ariane_testharness.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
